description: Sector overlay for software, SaaS, cloud, digital platforms, IT services, cybersecurity, privacy, anti-spam, consumer-facing digital products, and technology-service documentation in Canada.

metadata.scope: Canada-only sector overlay controls for Technology. Intended to be used with canada_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official Canadian federal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. Provincial/territorial obligations may apply and are not exhaustively encoded. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA)
Baseline cyber security controls for small and medium organizations
Top measures to enhance cyber security for small and medium organizations

rules.title: Privacy and personal-information controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for PIPEDA, privacy policy, consent, personal information, access/correction, retention, or processor controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: CASL electronic-message controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for CASL, commercial electronic message, express consent, unsubscribe, sender identification, or installation consent evidence.

metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Canadian legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, jurisdictional scope, provincial/territorial variations, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Baseline cybersecurity controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for MFA, backups, patching, endpoint protection, incident response, training, or access control evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.

rules.title: Cloud/data hosting and third-party processing controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for cloud provider, hosting, subcontractor, cross-border transfer, service provider, or security addendum evidence.
